senses.definition: a type of tennis that is played on sand with a special paddle and soft ball

examples.x: Sarah and Tom are champions at beach tennis; they never lose a match.
Let's grab our beach tennis gear and head to the shore!
The local beach club hosts weekly beach tennis tournaments in the summer.
Beach tennis requires quick reflexes due to the sandy court surface.
Beach tennis made its debut as an official sport at the 2005 Asian Beach Games.

senses.definition: a form of tennis adapted for players who use wheelchairs due to physical disabilities

examples.x: She excels at wheelchair tennis despite her physical challenges.
Wheelchair tennis allows players with disabilities to compete at a high level.
The championship features some of the best wheelchair tennis players in the world.
Wheelchair tennis tournaments attract large crowds of spectators.
She dreams of representing her country in wheelchair tennis at the Paralympic Games.

senses.definition: a stroke hit diagonally across the court from one corner to the opposite corner in tennis

examples.x: She executed a perfect cross-court shot to win the point.
Maria's cross-court shot landed just inside the line for a winner.
The cross-court shot is a useful tactic to move your opponent wide.
The cross-court shot opened up the court for a winning opportunity.

senses.definition: (tennis) the failure of a player to successfully serve the ball into the opponent's court twice in a row, resulting in the loss of a point

examples.x: She lost the game after committing a double fault on match point.
His nerves got the best of him, resulting in a double fault.
The player's frustration grew with each double fault.
The match turned in her favor after her opponent made a double fault.
Her opponent capitalized on the double fault, winning the game.

senses.definition: a basic tennis shot made after the ball bounces on the court, usually from the baseline

examples.x: She perfected her ground stroke during practice.
His powerful ground stroke won him many points.
Her backhand ground stroke is especially strong.
He used a topspin ground stroke to keep the ball in play.
She struggled with her ground stroke in the windy conditions.

senses.definition: a tennis shot aimed to get past an opponent at the net

examples.x: She won the point with a precise passing shot.
Practicing the passing shot improved her game.
The passing shot is crucial against net players.
He executed a perfect passing shot down the line.
A good passing shot can catch the opponent off guard.

senses.definition: the point in tennis scored after a tie, giving the player a chance to win the game with the next point

examples.x: She had the advantage and needed one more point to win.
She gained the advantage with a strong serve.
He lost the advantage with a double fault.
The game went back to deuce after losing the advantage.
The advantage was quickly lost due to an unforced error.

senses.definition: a situation in tennis where the receiving player can win the game if they win the next point against the server's serve

examples.x: He saved three break points in a row with powerful serves.
She seized the break point opportunity with a brilliant passing shot.
The pressure was on as he faced his opponent's break point.
Despite facing multiple break points, she held her serve with
The match turned in her favor after she converted a crucial break point.

senses.definition: a tie in tennis that requires a player to win two more points to win the game

examples.x: The match was at deuce, creating a tense atmosphere.
He focused intensely when the score hit deuce.
They exchanged several deuces before someone won.

senses.definition: the act of winning a game while receiving serve, instead of serving
(particularly in tennis) to strike a ball with such force as to make it impossible to return

examples.x: She killed the serve with a powerful forehand.
His opponent couldn't return the ball after he killed it with a strong shot.
The professional tennis player killed the rally with a precise backhand.
The top-ranked player killed the volley with a lightning-quick reaction.
She aimed for the corner and killed the shot with accuracy.
With a mighty swing, she killed the ball, sending it rocketing past her opponent.

senses.definition: (tennis) a quick and controlled movement of the wrist to generate spin on the ball during a shot

examples.x: She used a flick of her wrist to add topspin to her forehand.
His backhand flick caught his opponent off guard.
Maria's flick produced a sharp angle, making it difficult for her opponent to return.
The key to mastering the drop shot is a subtle flick of the wrist.
A well-timed flick can turn a defensive shot into an offensive opportunity.

senses.definition: a powerful shot with great force and speed, often seen in games like tennis or badminton

examples.x: The tennis player won the point with a powerful smash down the line.
His badminton smash caught his opponent off guard, scoring the winning point.
The volleyball player's smash was too strong for the opposing team to defend.
She executed a perfect overhead smash in the squash match.
The badminton player practiced his smash technique to improve his game.

senses.definition: the moment in tennis when a player needs one more point to win the match

examples.x: She nervously prepared to serve on match point.
The crowd held its breath as he approached match point.
With determination, she faced her opponent on match point.
He served aggressively to clinch match point.
She saved match point with a brilliant shot.

senses.definition: the act of angrily or intentionally damaging a tennis racket during a match

examples.x: The player received a warning from the umpire for racket abuse after smashing his racket against the court.
Racket abuse can result in point penalties or even disqualification from a match.
Serena Williams was fined for racket abuse during her match at the Grand Slam tournament.
His frustration led to an outburst of racket abuse, shattering his racket into pieces.
The player apologized to the crowd for his display of racket abuse and vowed to remain composed in future matches.

senses.definition: an unsuccessful serve attempt in tennis, where the ball does not land in the opponent's service box, resulting in the loss of a point

examples.x: He was frustrated after committing another fault.
She aimed to avoid faults by practicing her serve technique.
His first serve was a fault, so he tried again.
He struggled with his serve, resulting in many faults.

senses.definition: a situation in tennis where the leading player or team needs only one more point to win the game

examples.x: She remained focused as she approached game point in the final set.
It all comes down to this crucial game point to determine the winner.
The crowd erupted into cheers when he clinched the match on game point.
With a powerful serve, he secured game point and the advantage.
They fought tooth and nail to save game point and stay in the match.

senses.definition: a tennis shot where the player hits the ball immediately after it bounces, close to the ground

examples.x: He executed a perfect half volley to win the point.
Her half volley caught her opponent off guard.
A good half volley can neutralize a fast-paced return.
He was praised for his quick reflexes on the half volley.
Her opponent was surprised by the accuracy of her half volley.

senses.definition: a tennis shot hit with a slicing motion, often with backspin to keep the ball low

examples.x: She used a chop shot to surprise her opponent.
His chop shot stayed low over the net.
A well-executed chop shot can be tricky to return.
She learned the chop shot from her coach.
He practiced his chop shot for accuracy.

senses.definition: a type of tennis played with a softer ball and on a smaller court compared to traditional tennis

examples.x: Soft tennis is popular in Japan and other Asian countries.
She prefers soft tennis over regular tennis because of the lighter ball.
Have you ever played soft tennis before?
hey organized a soft tennis tournament at the local club.
He bought a new racket specifically for soft tennis matches.

senses.definition: the point in tennis that needs to be received by a player to win the set

examples.x: He celebrated after winning the set on set point.
She served for set point with confidence.
She focused intensely on winning set point.
Winning set point gave him a crucial lead.
He saved multiple set points with strong play.
